task_desc: Fix Brain3D WebGL init failure leaving an unrecoverable empty overlay and add null guards to hide() -- Two related error-handling gaps in brain3d.js show()/hide() that together can completely block the application on devices without WebGL support: (1) show() at brain3d.js:269 sets Brain3D._container.style.display = 'block' BEFORE calling init() at line 270; if init() fails inside its try/catch (WebGL unavailable, context limit exceeded on mobile where limits can be 8-16), the catch block at lines 179-183 sets _initialized = false and returns, then show() checks !_initialized at line 271 and returns early, but never resets the container display back to 'none'; since Brain3D.active remains false, the toggle() function at lines 294-300 keeps calling show() on subsequent button clicks (active is false so it enters the show branch), which retries init() and fails again, setting display to 'block' again each time; the user is stuck with an empty overlay (background 0x0a0a1a was never rendered since the WebGL renderer was never appended) permanently covering the main canvas with no UI path to dismiss it -- the button shows inactive state (aria-pressed=false, no active class) because active is false, yet the overlay div is visible; the only recovery is a page refresh; fix by adding Brain3D._container.style.display = 'none' before the early return at line 271, and add a _initFailed flag set in the catch block that prevents infinite retry attempts (show() should check _initFailed and return immediately without setting display to block). (2) hide() at brain3d.js:282-291 accesses Brain3D._renderer.domElement (line 284), Brain3D._container.style (line 285), and Brain3D._tooltipEl.style (line 287) without any null/undefined guards; while the normal toggle() path only reaches hide() when active is true (which requires successful init), the public API has no protection and future code changes (or console debugging) that call hide() before successful init will throw TypeError; add null guards (e.g. if (Brain3D._renderer) before domElement access, if (Brain3D._container) before style access) so hide() degrades gracefully. Files: js/brain3d.js.
